volumeTitle: The Woman of Andros. The Self-Tormentor. The Eunuch
label: The Woman of Andros (Section 4.4.747 - Section 4.4.795)

sections.label: Section 4.4.747
sections.original: Cur tu obsecro hic me solam ?
sections.translation: Pray , why did you leave me here alone ?

sections.label: Section 4.4.747b
sections.translation: Ha ! what story is this ? How now , Mysis , whence comes this child ? Who has brought it here ?

sections.label: Section 4.4.749
sections.original: Satin sanus es qui me id rogites ?
sections.translation: Are you quite right in your senses , to be asking me that ?

sections.label: Section 4.4.749b
sections.translation: Whom , then , ought I to ask ,

sections.label: Section 4.4.750
sections.translation: as I don ’ t see any one else here ?

sections.label: Section 4.4.750b
sections.translation: I wonder whence it has come .

sections.label: Section 4.4.751
sections.translation: Are you going to tell me what I ask ?

sections.label: Section 4.4.751b
sections.translation: Pshaw !

sections.label: Section 4.4.751c
sections.translation: Step aside to the right .

sections.label: Section 4.4.752
sections.translation: You are out of your senses ; didn ’ t you your own self ?

sections.label: Section 4.4.752b
sections.translation: Take you care not to utter a single word beyond what I ask you .

sections.label: Section 4.4.754b
sections.translation: Why don ’ t you say aloud whence it comes ?

sections.label: Section 4.4.754c
sections.translation: From our house .

sections.label: Section 4.4.754d
sections.translation: Heyday , indeed !

sections.label: Section 4.4.755
sections.original: Mirum vero impudenter mulier si facit
sections.translation: it really is a wonder if a woman , who is a courtesan , acts impudently .

sections.label: Section 4.4.756b
sections.original: Ab Andria est haec quantum intelligo .
sections.translation: So far as I can learn , this woman belongs to the Andrian .

sections.label: Section 4.4.757
sections.translation: Do we seem to you such very suitable persons for you to be playing tricks with us in this way ?

sections.label: Section 4.4.758b
sections.translation: I came just in time .

sections.label: Section 4.4.759
sections.original: Propera adeo puerum tollere hinc ab ianua :
sections.translation: Make haste then , and take the child away from the door here :

sections.label: Section 4.4.760
sections.original: Mane : cave quoquam ex istoc excessis loco .
sections.translation: ( in a low voice .) stay there ; take care you don ’ t stir from that spot .

sections.label: Section 4.4.761
sections.original: Di te eradicent : ita me miseram territas .
sections.translation: May the Gods confound you ! you do so terrify poor me .

sections.label: Section 4.4.762
sections.translation: Is it to you I speak or not ?

sections.label: Section 4.4.762b
sections.translation: What is it you want ?

sections.label: Section 4.4.762c
sections.translation: What — do you ask me again ? Tell me , whose child have you been laying here ? Let me know .

sections.label: Section 4.4.764
sections.translation: Don ’ t you know ?

sections.label: Section 4.4.764b
sections.original: Mitte id quod scio : dic quod rogo .
sections.translation: Have done with what I know ; tell me what I ask .

sections.label: Section 4.4.765
sections.translation: It belongs to your people .

sections.label: Section 4.4.765b
sections.translation: Which of our people ?

sections.label: Section 4.4.765c
sections.translation: To Pamphilus .

sections.label: Section 4.4.765d
sections.translation: How ? What — to Pamphilus ?

sections.label: Section 4.4.766
sections.translation: How now — is it not so ?

sections.label: Section 4.4.766b
sections.original: Recte ego semper fugi has nuptias .
sections.translation: With good reason have I always been averse to this match , it ’ s clear .

sections.label: Section 4.4.767
sections.translation: O abominable piece of effrontery !

sections.label: Section 4.4.767b
sections.translation: Why are you bawling out so ?

sections.label: Section 4.4.768
sections.original: Quemne ego heri vidi ad vos afferri vesperi ?
sections.translation: What , the very one I saw being carried to your house yesterday evening ?

sections.label: Section 4.4.769
sections.translation: O you impudent fellow !

sections.label: Section 4.4.769b
sections.translation: It ’ s the truth . I saw Canthara stuffed out beneath her clothes . [*]

sections.label: Section 4.4.770b
sections.translation: I ’ faith , I thank the Gods that several free women were present [*] at the delivery .

sections.label: Section 4.4.772
sections.original: Ne illa illum haud novit cuius causa haec incipit .
sections.translation: Assuredly she doesn ’ t know him , on whose account she resorts to these schemes . Chremes , she fancies , if he sees the child laid before the door , will not give his daughter ; i ’ faith , he ’ ll give her all the sooner .

sections.label: Section 4.4.775
sections.translation: I ’ faith , he ’ ll not do so .

sections.label: Section 4.4.775b
sections.original: Nunc adeo ut tu sis sciens ,
sections.translation: Now therefore , that you may be quite aware , if you don ’ t take up the child , I ’ ll roll it forthwith into the middle of the road ; and yourself in the same place I ’ ll roll over into the mud .

sections.label: Section 4.4.778
sections.original: Tu pol homo non es sobrius .
sections.translation: Upon my word , man , you are not sober .

sections.label: Section 4.4.778b
sections.translation: One scheme brings on another . I now hear it whispered about that she is a citizen of Attica —

sections.label: Section 4.4.780b
sections.translation: Ha !

sections.label: Section 4.4.780c
sections.translation: And that , constrained by the laws , [*] he will have to take her as his wife .

sections.label: Section 4.4.781b
sections.original: Au obsecro an non civis est ?
sections.translation: Well now , pray , is she not a citizen ?

sections.label: Section 4.4.782
sections.original: Iocularium in malum insciens paene incidi .
sections.translation: I had almost fallen unawares into a comical misfortune .

sections.label: Section 4.4.783
sections.original: Quis hic loquitur ? o Chreme , per tempus advenis .
sections.translation: Who ’ s that , speaking ? ( Pretending to look about .) O Chremes , you have come in good time . Do listen to this .

sections.label: Section 4.4.784b
sections.translation: I have heard it all already .

sections.label: Section 4.4.785b
sections.translation: Prithee , did you hear it ? Here ’ s villainy for you ! she ( pointing at MYSIS .) ought to be carried off [*] hence to the torture forthwith . ( To MYSIS , pointing at CHREMES .) This is Chremes himself ; don ’ t suppose that you are trifling with Davus only .

sections.label: Section 4.4.788
sections.original: Me miseram : nihil pol falsi dixi mi senex .
sections.translation: Wretched me ! upon my faith I have told no untruth , my worthy old gentleman .

sections.label: Section 4.4.789
sections.original: Novi rem omnem : est Simo intus ?
sections.translation: I know the whole affair . Is Simo within ?

sections.label: Section 4.4.789b
sections.translation: He is .

sections.label: Section 4.4.789c
sections.translation: Don ’ t touch me , villain . ( Moving away .) On my word , if I don ’ t tell Glycerium all this —

sections.label: Section 4.4.791
sections.original: Eho inepta nescis quid sit actum ?
sections.translation: How now , simpleton , don ’ t you know what has been done ?

sections.label: Section 4.4.791b
sections.translation: How should I know ?

sections.label: Section 4.4.792
sections.original: Hic socer est . Alio pacto haud poterat fieri
sections.translation: This is the bride ’ s father . It couldn ’ t any other way have been managed that he should know the things that we wanted him to know .

sections.label: Section 4.4.793b
sections.translation: You should have told me that before .

sections.label: Section 4.4.794
sections.original: Paullum interesse censes ex animo omnia ,
sections.translation: Do you suppose that it makes little difference whether you do things according to impulse ,

sections.label: Section 4.4.795
sections.original: Ut fert natura , facias an de industria ?
sections.translation: as nature prompts , or from premeditation ?
